title: Showcase + Gallery Walk
subtitle: present, explore, inspire!
overview: In this session, students present their STEAM projects in a creative showcase and participate in a gallery walk where they observe, learn, and appreciate the work of other teams. The activity develops confidence, communication skills, observation skills, and peer learning.

images._note: Add real photos from the showcase event — project displays, student presentations, poster displays, gallery walk moments, judges interaction, and group photos.

activity.title: STEAM Showcase + Gallery Walk
steps: Arrange all student projects neatly on display tables
Each team prepares a short 2-minute explanation
Students present their projects to classmates and teachers
Visitors walk around and observe different projects
Students ask questions and share feedback using sticky notes
Teachers appreciate creativity, teamwork, and innovation
End with reflection and group discussion

quiz.q: What is the main purpose of a Gallery Walk?
options: To observe and learn from different projects
quiz.explanation: A Gallery Walk helps students explore and learn from the projects created by others.

quiz.q: Why is communication important during a project showcase?
options: To explain ideas clearly and confidently
quiz.explanation: Good communication helps others understand your project and ideas.

quiz.q: What should students do while observing other projects?
quiz.explanation: Gallery Walks encourage curiosity, appreciation, and peer learning.

quiz.q: Which skill improves when students present projects publicly?
quiz.explanation: Presenting projects helps students become more confident speakers.

options: Working together to achieve a goal
quiz.explanation: Teamwork means collaborating and helping each other to complete a task.

quiz.q: What can students use to give feedback during the Gallery Walk?
quiz.explanation: Sticky notes are commonly used to write positive feedback and suggestions.

quiz.q: What should a good project presentation include?
quiz.explanation: A good presentation explains the idea clearly and demonstrates how it works.

options: Creating new ideas or improving existing ones
quiz.explanation: Innovation means thinking creatively and developing useful ideas or solutions.

quiz.q: How should students behave during a showcase event?
quiz.explanation: Respectful behavior creates a positive and enjoyable learning environment.

quiz.q: What is one benefit of observing other student projects?
quiz.explanation: Students gain inspiration and new learning ideas by observing others' work.
